name: Kucana v. Holder
question: Does 8 U.S.C. § 1252(a)(2)(B)(ii) bar judicial review for both administrative decisions made discretionary by statute and those made discretionary by regulation?
description: A case in which the Court held that 8 U.S.C. Section 1252(a)(2)(B)(ii) does not preclude federal courts from hearing cases specified by the Attorney General, only cases that are specified by statute.
